[h2]Sinopsis[/h2] GRIME es un fabuloso metroidvania con un estilo de combate tipo Souls, un sistema RPG y un universo/trama de lo más interesantes. GRIME trascurre en un mundo surrealista, decadente y grotesco, donde todo ser viviente está hecho de pura roca. La sociedad está fuertemente dividida, desde horrendas criaturas salvajes y seres humanoides deformados, hasta aristócratas esculpidos con preciosas formas y de gran poder. Nuestro protagonista es Akhlan, un poderoso individuo con un agujero negro por cabeza y un importante cometido, traer consigo la destrucción y renacimiento del mundo. Akhlan podría considerarse una especie de mesías y, al igual que ocurre con toda religión, encontraremos en nuestro camino fieles servidores y detractores de nuestra causa. Aquellos conocidos como los “Padres del Mundo” vaticinaron nuestra llegada y reacios a asumir el final, harán lo posible por pararnos los pies antes de que sea demasiado tarde.  [h2]Jugabilidad / Apartado técnico[/h2] La jugabilidad de GRIME es excelente y se siente muy bien pulida en todos los aspectos. Por un lado, tenemos el componente metroidvania, ya que podremos explorar un vasto mundo orgánico en 2D (pero con modelados en 3D), dividido en diferentes regiones a las cuales iremos ganando acceso conforme vayamos desbloqueando las distintas habilidades de Akhlan. El plataformeo cobra un papel fundamental en el desplazamiento y no son pocas las zonas donde sudaremos la gota gorda combinando todas nuestras habilidades para sortear los obstáculos, gracias al doble salto, atracción de objetos, planear en el aire, correr, impulsarnos, etc. Por otro lado, tenemos el sistema de combate inspirado en los Souls, con una barra de estamina (vigor) que tendremos que controlar para no quedar vendidos y un gran uso de los parries o defensas, que nos permitirán absorber y acabar con nuestros enemigos rápidamente. Todos los enemigos al morir soltarán materia, una sustancia fundamental que podremos utilizar para subir de nivel y mejorar nuestras características (vitalidad, vigor, fuerza, destreza, etc.), comprar nuevos objetos o potenciar nuestras armas. Como buen RPG, podremos equipar diferentes sets de armaduras y hasta 2 armas diferentes entre un completo arsenal, algunas de las cuales exigirán que tengamos mejorados ciertos atributos. Encontraremos diferentes tipos de enemigos o presas, algunos solo soltarán materia, mientras que otros nos proporcionarán puntos de mejora o nuevas habilidades en el caso de los jefes. Los combates con los jefes son espectaculares y exigentes, debiendo aprender sus mecánicas y movimientos para acabar con ellos. Tras completar el juego podremos acceder al NG+, empezando de nuevo la aventura con todos los atributos, habilidades, armas/armaduras y objetos que teníamos. Este modo resultará más complicado, todos los enemigos y bosses tendrán algunas mecánicas nuevas de combate y podremos mejorar ciertas habilidades y armas por encima de lo normal.  [h2]Valoración[/h2] GRIME es un metroidvania redondo y muy gratificante, que se siente repleto de contenido. La jugabilidad es rápida y precisa, haciendo que el plataformeo sea divertido y retador. El mundo es enorme, con múltiples zonas, cada una con sus enemigos y peligros particulares, llenas de secretos y rincones ocultos. Gráficamente luce muy bien, con un tono oscuro y decadente que encaja con la historia, la cual resulta interesante, aunque algo compleja de pillar al principio, pues se habla de muchos personajes o entidades que iremos conociendo muy progresivamente. Los combates son complicados y castigan cualquier error, por lo que tendremos que aprender a controlar la estamina y el sistema de parries lo antes posible. A pesar de ello, los jefes tienen una dificultad asequible y no son tan frustrantes como en otros juegos. Por si fuera poco, el juego ha ido creciendo a base de DLCs gratuitos que han añadido nuevas zonas, mecánicas, armas y habilidades con el tiempo, algo muy de agradecer teniendo en cuenta que la mayoría de juegos obligan a pagar continuamente por el nuevo contenido. Si te molan los metroidvanias, GRIME es una parada obligatoria.  ✔️ [b]Positivo[/b]:[list] [*]Universo e historia muy interesantes, donde encarnaremos al mesías de la destrucción de un mundo decadente y oscuro, socialmente dividido y habitado por seres hechos de roca.  [*]Jugabilidad excelente y fluida, tanto en las fases de plataformeo como en los complejos combates tipo Souls. A nivel gráfico también resulta muy atractivo. [*]Una cantidad inmensa de contenido que ha ido creciendo a base de DLCs gratuitos. Además del enorme mapa, posee un sistema RPG muy cuidado, múltiples habilidades, armas/armaduras y criaturas a las que dar caza. [/list] ❌ [b]Negativo[/b]:[list] [*]Dado el estilo Souls de sus combates, el juego puede ser desafiante y frustrante, pudiendo morir fácilmente contra la criatura más débil o penosa si no esquivamos o desviamos bien el ataque.  [*]La trama resulta muy interesante y trabajada, pero puede resultar algo confusa por la gran cantidad de entidades y personajes que se mencionan, a veces fuera de contexto.  [*]Aunque el NG+ ofrece mucho contenido optimizado y un mayor nivel de dificultad, no soy especialmente fan de tener que volver a pasarme un juego largo para completar el 100% (pero lo hago igualmente jaja). [/list]  📢 [b]Idioma[/b]: Interfaz y subtítulos en español. ⌛ [b]Duración[/b]: 20 horas juego base/ 35-40 horas para 100%. 🎮 [b]Steam Deck[/b]: Verificado, funciona perfectamente. Life or Death, Consume or Be Consumed, this is all that matters.  Grime is a Soulslike Metroidvania that makes a few key changes to the formula that make playing the game far different, and generally more interesting, than most of its contemporaries.   The two mechanics that really change things up are tied to the same resource, Ardor.  Shortly after starting the game, this mechanic will be explained.  As you kill enemies, and especially when you parry and absorb them, you gain ardor.  The higher your ardor, the more "mass" you obtain, but being hit lowers your Ardor.  Mass is the material you use to level up, purchase items, and upgrade weapons, so it's very important, so keeping your Ardor high make the game generally easier.  When you die, rather than losing your "mass", you lose all your accumulated Ardor instead, and making it back to where you died allows you to regain half of what you lost (which can become all of what you lost with upgrades).  So, unlike most soulslike games, there is no risk of losing all your hard earned "souls", or mass in this case, by dying repeatedly.  The ease and relatively low Ardor cap also means that you don't have a high incentive to retrieve your lost Ardor, and therefor, little incentive to just try the same path or boss over and over again.  If you are turned off of Soulslike games due to the ability to permanently lose hard earned resources, this game solves that problem.  Outside of this major change, this game plays just like you'd expect.  The combat is simple, but gains complexity over time.  You're highly rewarded for learning enemy and boss moves, how to dodge them, parry them, or use special moves on them, but making mistakes won't kill you right away in most cases.  With all this in mind, this is still an incredibly difficult game, both in platforming and combat, so don't get into this if you don't enjoy that.  This game has a ton of content for its price, and has plenty of secrets to sink your teeth into, but this breadth of content is also a bit of an issue.  To access everything the game has to offer, you HAVE to start a Newgame+.  You'll earn new rewards for defeating bosses and finding secrets that allow you to upgrade your character past the previous max, BUT, you'll have to face all the same foes you already did with them all being more difficult, but without the "mass" rewards being increased.  This means that you're going to be short on resources and need to replay the majority of the game to see some new bosses and events.  Personnaly, I think this is too much of a time investment to be worth it to me.  I like the game, and think it's a good time, but I have no interest in replaying it with and even higher difficulty just to see a little extra content.  However, if you end up loving the game, then this second playthrough will be the icing on the cake for you.

I'd give Grime a 7.5/10, it's a pretty solid Metroidvania, with a couple of big flaws.  The game has an abnormal difficulty curve, you get access to the  3rd, 4th, and 5th bosses at the same time, and they are far harder than the rest of the bosses in game. They are far too unforgiving with their strange attack patterns, and you fight them when you have very small health pool, and practically no abilities.   Your inventory is pretty cluttered too, I really hate how armor system works. They come in sets of 3 pieces (Torso, Arms, Legs), where you can mix and match them for various stats. The problem is you get a very substantial boost for equipping the same pieces in a set, so I found it pointless to have them all as unique pieces, just adds needless inventory space. Music is completely forgettable, but it's inoffensive at least.  With that being said, the aesthetics in terms of monster designs are genuinely great. Bosses are for the most part pretty fun outside of the three I mentioned, and fairly challenging. If you like the aesthetics from the trailer, and you're prepared to challenge yourself, I think you'll enjoy your time with Grime.

一款体验很割裂的游戏，但战斗做的确实好，长板很长，所以这个差评给不下手。 说割裂是因为前期真的很受苦，角色初始就是个残疾人，连跑步都不会，地图还有各种各样的恶意，环境伤害高，怪不小心引到两个以上就不好搞，有些精英怪伤害高得夸张，回血还只能靠弹反，初见很容易死，更别提指引一坨，得到处找路，关键是这游戏还很能藏，有些地图开图点藏得贼深，还有些隐藏道路也是藏得贼深。前期还有经典的直通后期地图的岔路，绕了半天找不到椅子也找不到开图点，看到怪两下嗝屁，然后只能灰溜溜的跑路。综合下来至少前中期的跑图体验我觉得用“灾难”两个字形容并不夸张。说是类银，但说实话有知名度的类银我也玩了不少，但跑图体验比这游戏差的我真想不出来，感觉与其说是类银不如以魂游的预期来玩这个游戏。 但是吧，当你玩到中期，角色属性上来了，学新能力了，有厉害的武器了，回血手段变多了之后，角色就从残疾人变成赛亚人了，到后期推图基本是开无双，打BOSS也是容错率贼高，前期很多BOSS能卡我半个小时以上，后期反而有两个BOSS我一次就过了，这时候就真是类银了，而且你会发现这游戏类银元素做的还挺扎实，藏东西反而成推图的正反馈了。 所以吧，我对这游戏的感情很复杂，一方面是前期体验确实很糟糕，实打实被恶心到了，另一方面，打BOSS又确实很爽，包括有些压力很大的精英怪我也觉得打得很爽。另外，我不反感跳跳乐，这游戏的跳跳乐我感觉难度做得比较适中，甚至还有grime版苦痛之路，在我这算是加分项。 音乐不错，美术见仁见智，我个人不太喜欢这种画风，噢对了，这游戏很多场景很暗，看多了眼睛不舒服，建议进去把亮度调高点，能够改善游戏体验。 推荐给深度类银爱好者或者有魂游经验的玩家，现在这个史低价格入手性价比是真挺高的。但如果你是轻度玩家，或者说不想受苦的话，其实我更推荐育碧这两年出的那款类银，和这游戏真挺像的，价格要贵一些，但不受苦，玩起来要比这游戏流畅。（叠个甲，这真不是拉踩） 最后，如果你前期迷路了，或者被跑图折磨的不行了，建议该看攻略看攻略，攻略帮我度过了中期最难熬的一段时间，不然我可能真撑不过去。

К сожалению, документ с моим "сочинением" был утерян, так что буду кратка. Игра мне понравилась, новая игра+ нет. Имбаланс огромный, я б даже сказала неоправданный. Локация Литит слишком однообразна и запутана в секции под землёй. Без карты я там ориентироваться не могу. Предполагаю, что если б камера отдалялась подальше, в этой локации, то было б не так плохо. Что ещё. Было у меня 3 раза, когда я застревала и не понимала где мне взять способность, что бы дальше пройти. Помню только первый раз, остальные я сразу шла смотреть прохождение. Первый раз был, когда маркер квеста указывал вниз, в сады или колыбель, а надо было пойти в эту башню обтёсанных(или как их там) и победить босса. :( Я вижу, что многим не нравится локации с паркуром, а мне они очень даже зашли. Даже было чувство "и что? это всё???", так что думаю что негодовали те игроки, которых привлёк тэг "Souls like", а не "Metroidvania". Знаю, что написала больше про минусы, а игру советую, но мне немного до фени. Лор интересный, персонажи и боссы запоминающиеся, моим главным оружием были парные ручные клинки где-то с начала игры, общая философия и финальный выбор дали мне пищу для размышлений. Слава космическим родителям, что разработчики добавили телепорт через точки сохранения, аминь.

I recommend this game, I have finished it, here are the flaws/cons and the things I liked/pros that I found. They will be listed below   1. In this game at least 30% of your stamina is used to literally just dodge. Meaning hard early-mid game 2. The difficulty spike in different biomes is insane, like the first 2 desert maps are nothing compared to the "childbed" biome (After finishing the game for the first time I realize that ♥♥♥♥ was DLC biome, that would explain the difficulty, but I still stand on the difficulty spike being way too much) 3. buggy, game been out for years now yet there are still animation bugs, like visible enemy models through walls like in the desert biome or lithic where the rockheads are visible through their ambush spots, climbing ladders where the weapon is shown, minor nitpick here but I prefer having that realistic feel of the endgiver having "physical no weapon model sticking to his hand" feel when climbing ladders. I believe this also only happens when dashing/sprinting and immediately grabbing on a ladder. 4. Some attacks are hard to guess whether they can be parried or not(I know glowing red means cannot be parried, but I am referring to some enemies and boss moves) a good example of this is the boss fight on the childbed biome the Giant of Eye boss took me an actual hour cause of my issue in no. 1(stamina) and I deadass cannot tell if I can parry some of his attacks considering the guy is a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ giant. 5. Terrible remnant acquisition, unlike the souls game, in this game for which I will  also praise your currency(mass) does not lose upon death, however you do still leave a remnant, now this remnant will heal you for a set percentage, meaning that can be used on hard boss fights, now here's the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ issue. To acquire it you NEED to hit it not even just interact with it meaning you can accidentally hit your free extra heal on a hard boss, it ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ sucks, it would have been better if it restores some of you breath(estus flask in dark souls) but no the main issue here is the fact that acquisition activates on HIT 6. At first I though that not being able to map out the area you're in is unique you need to find a shard that reveals that biomes map, like that is unique right, then a few hours into this mess I came to realize how annoying it is to do that every ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ time especially when a bunch of enemies are blocking the direct path that leads to that shard  7. Not having the ability to use an item in the ITEM MENU, you always have to assign an item to the four slots before having the ability to use said items  8. Multiple weapons lacking more movesets or are just plain uninteresting to use, I'll give two examples; first, both the spine sword and spine axe have the same gimmick the ONLY real difference is the damage(scaling) and moveset of the axe being better considering it attacks always on long range. My second example is the Discarded tool and the Bone grinder, both literally having the exact same moveset with the only real difference this time is that the Bone grinder much better at dealing damage.  Pros/What I like: 1. Interesting plot, I'm actually invested although it is very cryptic at times  2. Nailed the soulslike feel/vibe unlike most soulslike game  3. Overcoming enemies by mastering specific playstyles is extremely rewarding and so is discovering secrets like new weapons and armor sets 4. Awesome soundtrack and visuals, I really like the aesthetic of this game; the 2D but also 3D-like background the monster and world designs, and obviously the main character, genuinely the only reason I got interested in this game is because I saw some random trailer way back then, then it has the main character having a black hole for a head   This is my updated version of my original review, I'm on my way to NG+ and hopefully be able to do a full achievement on this game

Felt off about it in the first hours, like a soulslike with a cheap yet solid combat system, very nice looking level design and a honestly beyond esoteric story. Now that I beat it, I can say with all my heart GRIME 2 is one of the only Games that I look forward to with little to no worry.  GRIME may start off a little rocky, but ends on such a high note I can't help but see it as a Masterpiece. In Hindsight, it's amazing to see [spoiler] that even that feeling of "I don't know what setting this is or what world I'm in, but I guess I'll start by killing things" is entirely explained in the lore throughout and especially towards the end. [/spoiler]  It's much more of a metroidvania type game with constantly evolving combat system, that all beautifully peaks at the last two Boss fights [spoiler] The Final Performance is one of the most beautiful intertwinings of combat and story I've seen to Date, even the concept of the Coda itselfis fckin genius and its my headcanon that they originally used to rebel against Aklahn but seeing as there simply is no point, they became as they are. [/spoiler] The story starts to make some sense about halfway through, and it becomes pretty clear towards the end if you've started to pay attention by that halfway point. An absolute Masterpiece in my honest opinion. Even better, the Currency to level up doesn't get dropped on death, only the multiplier resets. Which you get half back of when you reclaim your dying vessel.  I could glaze so much more over all it's aspects, but I believe most fun came from experiencing it first hand myself, so I won't steal any more joy of discovery than I might already have. It's more than worth it at full price and an absolute steal on sale.  Edit: The new Game plus exclusive Boss is also absolute Top Tier and gives me a lot of Tabu vibes from Smash Bros Brawl. Again, 10/10

Grime – Review  Grime has a really interesting concept and a unique style that makes it stand out among Metroidvania games. The world design, atmosphere, and combat system are all creative and well done.  That being said, it didn’t fully click with me personally — something about it just didn’t grab me the way other games in the genre do. Still, I can see the quality and effort behind it, and it definitely deserves a positive review.  I’m really hoping Grime 2 ends up being more my style, because the potential here is huge.
The graphics and controls feel a bit odd, but it has a nice appeal.  Hoping to play through it all.

Must-play for fans of the demanding action-platformer genre.  The world of Grime is unrelentingly dark, creepy, and utterly amazing. Every zone is crafted with a grotesque, organic aesthetic that oozes atmosphere. The narrative is presented in fragments, forcing you to piece together the lore of a bizarre, dying world.   The combat is challenging but incredibly rewarding. Enemies hit hard and require you to master a unique "Absorption" mechanic alongside traditional parries and dodges. Every victory feels earned. The platforming is equally engaging and difficult, often requiring precision and quick reflexes. I frequently caught myself punching the air and laughing in that mix of frustration and triumph after a near-miss or finally clearing a particularly difficult sequence. Earning the Coda Scythesword was a brutal marathon of white-knuckle anxiety—the kind that makes you lurch in your chair, desperately trying to will Akhlan towards the next grab-able surface.  Highly recommended.

It's an interesting subterranean Metroidvania with a focus on parrying and very light soulslike gameplay mechanics. Everything works well together: exploration is fun and rewarding, a few different ways to build and stat up your character, able to absorb enemies and use them for stat boosts, upgrade scalable weapons, etc.  If you enjoy Metroidvanias with customizable builds and often unforgiving difficulty, this game is definitely worth checking out.

It took, like, 5 tries over 3 years to overlook the sluggish, visually underwhelming, punishing first two areas and clunky UI to finally understand how to play. But once I did, I fell in love, and am so glad I stuck with this game.  Pros: -DIFFICULTY: Hard but fair, and fun to overcome. -GAMEPLAY: The black hole-themed abilities are aesthetically pleasing and gratifying to pull off. Had a blast parrying, pulling, dashing, and hovering my way through this weird world of living stone. Mastering difficult platforming sections provided plenty of items, fun exploration, and story progression. Weapon-based combat made me feel powerful, intelligently balancing light and heavy attacks throughout encounters. -CUSTOMIZATION: The game provides a diverse range of weapons to choose from with various shapes, speeds, and effects to suit individual playstyles. Leveling up your character's 5 stats (Health, Force, Strength, Dexterity, Resonance) and plethora of traits can be overwhelming at first, but thanks to Google and a respec system, I finally found a stat spread that felt right to me. Furthermore, a wealth of armour sets complement your character with stat and Trait-boosting effects, and any piece from a set can be equipped as a purely cosmetic item on top of the piece you equipped for its bonuses. -STORY: Love the cryptic lore told through vague dialogue hints and environmental storytelling. The game encourages interpretation, piecing together the clues of why the world is the way it is yourself. Perhaps it was a bit too mysterious, but I'll take that over too much telling-over-showing. I did find Goldhead's inclusion to have no real conclusion, which was unfortunate. I thought they'd become a cool boss to fight or something. :( -GRAPHICS: Initial areas and enemies can be a bit boring to look at (captivating body horror notwithstanding), but cutscenes, bosses, and later areas and enemies are gorgeous. The Surrogate Vulture fight was awe-inspiring in all its majesty in tandem with its boss theme. I did have to lower the graphics settings to improve game performance unfortunately, but normal graphics are still perfectly serviceable. -PERFORMANCE: Animations were fluid for the most part. My laptop may not be the strongest as I experienced stuttering when it wasn't plugged in. I've also experienced (very infrequent) bugs where the main character got stuck in walls, especially when fighting Surrogate Vulture through Prey Gauntlet on NG+. None of it was game-breaking, though, as I could easily destroy my vessel or restart the game from where I last left off. -SOUND: Soundtrack is fantastic. Alex Roe's hauntingly beautiful orchestral pieces suit the wondrous, brutal world of Grime. Favourite tracks include Surrogate Vulture, Grieving Rockgiant, and Dreamborn Terror. Ambient and enemy noises made the world feel alive, attacks sounded powerful and visceral, etc… -DIALOGUE: Spotted occasional typos throughout the game. Apart from that, dialogue is well-written overall, and I actually chuckled a few times!  Cons: -START OF GAME: It was kinda weak between the powerlessness of your character, the dour first few areas (my monkey brain likes vivid colours / nature / bodies of water over wholly inorganic areas I guess lol), and the clunky UI. I can see it turning people off as it did for me. -BACKTRACKING: It was frustrating until all Nervepasses and the ability to warp between Surrogates were unlocked.  Overall, I highly recommend this game to Metroidvania fans, Soulslike fans, and basically anyone who wants to play as a cool black hole man in a unique, lovingly crafted world. I initially thought I'd never beat this game, but I just beat the hardest boss today, and looking back on how far I've come makes me proud. Looking forward to Grime 2!

If you want a metroidvania with dark souls mechanics you should get grime. The atmosphere and and the music is just amazing. The ambient soundtrack is created by sean secca and has a haunting quality. There is also a guy called alex roe who did the orchestral boss music similar to what you would find a souls game. As you explore and kill prey you gain mass which you can spend at a surrogate to level up. Mass is the same as souls just with another name attached to it. You can also absorb prey just as they attack and after absorbing enough of the same enemy type you can unlock traits which are passive bonuses. The surrogate is basically just the bonfire from dark souls and it serves both as a respawn point and the place where you level up your stats and traits. You don't lose your mass when you die as you do in dark souls so you aren't punished for exploring new areas and looking for secrets. The game is still pretty hard but this design choice is a great change from the dark souls formula. I'm only around five and a half hour into the game but i'm having a great time so far and i highly recommend this game!

冷门佳作，这游戏耐力循环和弹反手感一绝。到世界之柱前因为续航问题会有一点受苦不过还好。早期被诟病的传送问题也已经解决（前中期到达世界之柱，完成施德拉给的任务打败boss秃鹫后，可以一路向下去到子体温床，打败boss大眼即可解锁传送）。各方面设计很有魂味，音乐美术也极佳。  被动设计也很优秀，对战斗血量和耐力循环有很强的正反馈。推荐各个用得到的被动都先点一级，性价比高，潜伏者的弹反吸血可以多点两级。  地图稍有欠缺，隐藏道路和正常道路之间连接部分不在大地图上显示，前期即使用得上隐藏道路也经常找不到很尴尬。大部分隐藏可以靠狂岩被动提示隐藏墙找出，但是还有部分隐藏藏的太深了，不看攻略很难找到。跳跳乐倒是不算难，主角机动性很高，镰型剑的跳跳乐比渎神联动5简单一些。顺便一提，易碎道路要靠爬行者被动慢走通过，慢走是在走动过程中按对应按键切换的这个有点反人类。  如果还是觉得受苦，武器可以用贪食者巢穴拿到的颌斧，比较超模，被动可以把疯石的弹反判定延长点高一点。

The platforming in this game is bad - I honestly would rank the platforming physics as the worst out of the hundreds of games I have ever played.  GRIME is an apt name for this game because it feels like you are moving through sludge, with every movement being slow to start and your character often sliding around out of your control.  It was so bad I was ready to uninstall within an hour, but I pushed through to the final boss with the singular goal of writing this review after experiencing everything the game had to offer.  Combat is poorly designed.  This is one of the few platformers where you just aren't supposed to move or jump in combat.  Combat alternates between two phases: phase one you stand still then press the parry button before an enemy hits you.  Phase two you mash attack while the enemy is recoiled.  Sometimes you have to press the dodge button instead of the parry button.  Some later game enemies and bosses require you to parry twice in phase one before going to phase two.  It does not feel satisfying at all, and does not feel like I am even controlling my character, more like I am doing quicktime events in an on-rails cutscene.  None of the bosses felt like they required me to get better at any game mechanics, they were mostly just learning what animations to dodge and how long after an animation to wait before pressing parry.  The music was also bad.  The boss music had decent moments, but I ended up turning the music volume to zero after 10 hours just because the background music in regular zones was so consistently awful.  Most of the environments look very similar as well, with 'rocks or dark dirt' describing 80% of the game.  I found the ideas of the different biomes interesting and liked some of the things I saw, but I think the execution ended up creating a dark, unmemorable mess that all blends together.  Ironically, the sections of the game that were the most enjoyable ended up being the platforming challenges that relied mostly on using airborne abilities - when you don't interact with platforms, the game can be somewhat fun to move around in.  But I would not say it is worth it to slog through everything else to experience these small challenges.  I love metroidvanias.  I enjoy difficult games.  I have hundreds of hours in Elden Ring.  I blasted through Silksong on release before any nerfs.  The only way to enjoy this game is to go in with the knowledge that the platforming is going to be garbage, so if that doesn't bother you, then go for it.

Very strong Metroidvania, 9.5/10 - I can't wait for Grime 2!  I liked: - A lot of content, it took me 30 hours to finish (without DLC and the bonus modes you unlock after beating the game) - Big atmospheric world with fun and rewarding exploration, and some nice interconnections. Some secrets take some trial and error to figure out, but it was always apparent that something must be there. There was no random BS that you can only figure out with a guide. - Good mini-map with the option to set custom markers (Mark the NPCs that you meet!) - Movement and combat feel good - I was a bit skeptical about this due to the 2.5D engine, but it plays as smooth as any pure 2D MV. - Very well balanced difficulty all around (mobs, bosses, platforming) - the game is challenging and you won't just ease your way through it, but there was no point where it wasn't fun anymore. If you hate games like Hollow Knight/Nine Sols/Aeterna Noctis for their time-wasting, overly punitive design (like I do) - don't worry. For me, this game here hit the fine line between an enjoyable challenge and frustration. - Upgradable weapons and multiple armor slots (including set bonuses), and many different unlockable buffs. Re-speccing of buffs and your main stats is possible with items that you find (and can also buy from a NPC) - Runs well, no performance issues, no crashes, and the possibilty to self-destruct and respawn at tha last save point in case you get stuck somewhere  I disliked: - The ability to teleport between save points is unlocked too late. Until then you'll have to do quite some walking because the save points can be pretty far from each other - typical "soulslike" NPC/quest design, i.e.: Everyone just talks cryptical BS and you have no clue what to do most of the time. But it never hindered the progression so I just didn't care too much about it. - You find new weapons and armor throughout the entire game, but you will rarely change your equipment because nothing is "better" than anything else, and you'll just upgrade pretty much the first things you find and keep them - You can get to regions where you're not supposed to be at yet because normally you would first need a certain ability, but the map design allows you to reach them anyway if you try hard enough. This can be confusing because you might not realize there's another path you should take first - the steam achievements seem to be bugged. I got achievements like "all abilities unlocked" or "all hunter points collected", but then still unlocked new abilities and collected more hunter points. So if you get those achievements but still wonder how to access certain areas in the map, don't worry, just keep playing and you'll get more abilities.  For further reference, below are some of my favorite MVs and I would put Grime right into this list: - Ori 1+2 - Ender Lillies/Ender Magnolia - BioGun - Afterimage - Awaken - Astral Blade - Voidwrought - Prince of Persia Lost Crown

不输空洞骑士的类银游戏，甚至有些地方超越，比如地图。 地图设计很好，探索体验像是魂一，篝火很少且前期无法传送全靠近路和优秀的地图设计，地图也和空洞骑士一样需要逐步解锁，但难点是有些地图你甚至探索完后才能解锁，好在就算地图未解锁也能看到自己的移动路径。 战斗设计也是亮点，可以通过弹反直接吞噬掉小型敌人，或者吞噬掉大型敌人的部位，比如吃掉螃蟹的钳子，多手敌人的手臂之类的，我很喜欢。但缺点也很明显，回血需要能量，但是前期能量基本上只能通过弹反获得，这就导致，你因为要回血去弹反，但是弹反失误又导致掉血，就恶性循环了。 但这个游戏是没有掉魂机制的。 2周目变化的不单单是敌人的数值，小怪和boss都会加新的招式，这个方面还没多少游戏可以做得到，开一次新周目新鲜感还是挺足的。而且2周目还会多一个boss难度非常高打的很爽。 美术方面也很不错，部分场景的环境音还真挺吓人的。  最大的缺点是这个游戏隐藏太多了，多到为了隐藏而去隐藏，下到随处可见的垃圾，上到重要的强化道具，都在藏。 拿空洞骑士举例子，空洞隐藏也就是点换金道具，最重要的也就是那些小青虫了，但这游戏属于是把空洞骑士的面具碎片，护符槽这种东西都在藏。  游戏质量不低，但隐藏多到恶心人，你不看攻略就会少非常多的东西，虽然不会影响到你通关，但让我感觉不爽，你看了攻略那种地图柳暗花明又一村的感觉就没了。
比较成熟的银河城了，用吸收替代弹反挺有意思的，整体有难度但是好玩。

GRIME is what you get when the madness of cosmic, unknowable horrors meets the brutality of (cliched, I know) Dark Souls in 2D. No two areas in this game are the same, there's always some bodily abomination you encounter, like a stag made of a jawbone, faces of stone in the background, or something so inhuman, you can't help but be intrigued. The combat, parrying especially, is so satisfying to master, and rewarding to absorb the powers of your foes to grow stronger and better as you explore the twisted landscape. Definitely worth a go if you love twisted visuals and simple-but-rewarding combat. My only hope is that GRIME II comes out soon, this developer really knows their stuff, and I want to see what other insane ideas they come up with.
Jesus lord, save us from the endless onslaught of Souls-likes infecting every other genre. Another slog of grimdark split second parries. It's a nightmare.

メトロイドヴァニア+ソウル。適当にソウルライクタグをつけているわけではなく、しっかりソウルライク。 実績コンプにはNG+までクリアが必要。 死にゲー寄りではあるが、同じボスで数時間詰まるようなことはない……と思う。  [h3]特徴[/h3] [list] 	[*]多くの攻撃が吸収（パリィ）可能で、システムとしてもそれを主軸に据えている 	[*]生命/持久/筋力/技量/魔法みたいなパラメータ振りがあり、武器の装備条件にも影響する 	[*]特定の敵を一定数吸収で倒すと対応した特性が解禁され、ポイントを振ることで発動できる（体力増加、吸収成功で回復、など） [/list]  [h3]アスレチックに関して[/h3] クリアに必須なアスレチックもあるが、そこまで難しくはない。白の宮殿よりも遥かに簡単で短い。 実績コンプリートとなると、それなりに難しいアスレチックエリアのクリアが必要となる。とは言ってもこちらもそこまで理不尽難易度ではなく、30分～1時間程度の長さだと思う。 いずれにしても、そこまで高難易度ではなく、メイン要素でもない。でもアレルギーがある人はやめておいた方が無難。  [h3]メトロイドヴァニアによくある機能の有無[/h3] [list] 	[*]マップ: 各エリアごとに、ビーコンを起動すると通った場所の近辺がマッピングされる。 	[*]ピン機能: あり。 	[*]ファストトラベル: 拠点へのテレポートは消費アイテムで可能。拠点間のテレポートは中盤～終盤で解禁。しかし拠点がまばらで、一方通行の道も結構あるので探索はかなりめんどくさい。 	[*]未探索のチェック機能: 無い。マップの端がちゃんと壁になっていなければまだ先があると推定はできる、程度。 	[*]隠し部屋: かなりある。普通にすり抜けられる場所、ダッシュならすり抜けられる場所、見た目で判別できないが攻撃すると壊れる壁、など多岐にわたる。ノーヒントで全て見つけるのは無理だと思う。 	[*]シーケンスブレイク: 多分無い。 [/list]  [h3]気になった点[/h3] [list] 	[*]移動速度も遅くテレポートも不便なので、探索がものすごく不便 	[*]暗くてよく見えないエリアがややある 	[*]スタミナゲーすぎる 	[*]後半になるにつれて、吸収不可な攻撃がどんどん増えていく 	[*]ノーヒントの隠し通路が多すぎる 	[*]ストーリーがさっぱり理解できなかった（たぶん翻訳自体はしっかりしているが、根本的に内容が難しいのではないか……） [/list]  それなりに戦闘が難しく、探索欲求を満たせる良いゲーム。

[h1][b]大疫情时代催生的，沉溺于绝望的悲观叙事[/b][/h1]  作为一款类银河恶魔城游戏，尘埃异变（grime）以其独特的艺术风格和别具特色的战斗体验，让我印象深刻。然而，在这具惊艳的“生物躯壳”之内，却跳动着一颗悲观而冰冷的心脏——这也是我给差评的原因，并非游戏性，而是因为立意。  [h1]先说优点和特色[/h1]  视觉效果上，游戏内所有场景脱胎于黑暗化的人体组织器官，不同地图能很明显的区分出消化道神经中枢皮肤耳鼻口等等，充斥着邪魅诡异以及一种别样的吸引力。  战斗操作上，游戏内的格挡判定非常宽松，加上一套独特的“拉扯”系统，使得战斗非常流畅顺滑。虽然战斗资源只有精力槽一项，闪避攻击都需要消耗，但在升级过技能后格挡和拉扯甚至闪避，都在一定条件下可以恢复精力，让防守和进攻浑然一体。BOSS战见招拆招，你可以打的行云流水连绵不绝，宛如一场华丽的舞蹈，非常有观赏性和成就感。  [h1]再说缺点和败笔[/h1]  首先是地图，使用了很传统的，在未找到信标前探索全黑的设计，说实话这种过时的困难真没必要在现如今还要强加给玩家。很多地图又过于黑暗，没有照明手段，加之游戏里有大量的隐藏元素，在如此黑不溜秋的环境下专注探索并不愉快，费神费心费力。  角色的性能很强，但是获得这些性能的途径和节点却不大合理，像二段跳滑翔空中冲刺拉扯物体等探图关键能力，要到游戏很后期才逐步解锁，但是初期地图却有很多需要这类能力的隐藏要素，甚至是近路的开启，让人摸不着头脑。有一些让战斗便捷的技能，比如我之前说的拉扯回精力闪避回精力等等，也要到中期地图吸收对应小怪才会给。地图攻略顺序也没有指引，初期就能误入难道较高的中后期地图，打的那叫一个受罪。存档点的传送功能要到中后期的地图打BOSS才能解锁，之前全靠双腿跑。想我玩的时候在20多级误入高等级地图，然后又缺少能力出不来，越级刮一个BOSS刮了好几天，是真的痛苦。  最最最让人恼火的是，游戏制作者每次版本更新，都会改动不少，一个游戏不应该在正式版改动的东西，小到BOSS技能，大到比如地图结构，支线任务安排，NPC位置等等。这让很多社区攻略迅速过时，文不对版，尤其是100%地图探索，武器装备全收集，当你对着攻略地图看的一头雾水的时候，就想问问制作组，你们这么改，不应该挂一个“抢先体验”吗？怎么就当正式版卖了呢？好在，目前游戏版本已经固定，新玩家不用招这罪了。不过网上查找攻略，记得找最新的版本。  抛去最后那个缺点，整体而言，我认为尘埃异变（grime）是个很不错的游戏，有自己的特色，内容丰富，战斗顺滑，虽然有缺点，但瑕不掩瑜，值得推荐，游戏性上我是要给出好评的。  [h1]就像开篇我说的那样，我之所以给出了差评，则是因为他的立意。[/h1]  尘埃异变发售于21年，其制作正处于全球疫情最压抑的阶段。游戏故事发生在巨大的人体内，开篇因为亲密接触借由呼吸道感染，出生地哭泣腔体以及前两个BOSS也都意指口鼻眼等黏膜组织，一路穿过消化道，神经系统，皮肤，骨骼等地图，最后突破免疫系统，借由脊髓侵入大脑。玩家操作的角色在过程中不断吞噬变异，吸收进化，变的无比强大，破坏途经的每一个器官，免疫系统徒劳的试图阻挡，但最终都一一沦陷。游戏的结局，大脑损坏，宿主死亡，病毒扩散，可以看到巨大空间里已经有大量因此死亡的人体，主角也将寻找下一个受害者重复如此的循环。  不过一千个人眼里有一千个哈姆雷特，grime所展现的在其他人眼里也可能是不同的故事。但在我看来，开发者将人类对病毒的恐惧转化为游戏中的毁灭寓言，充满了过度悲观的色彩。虽然病毒确实非常强大，它们变异迅速，传染悄无声息，造成了大量死亡。疫苗、药物也总是迟来一步，免疫系统奋力挣扎却最终徒劳无功，人类个体在病毒面前显得脆弱又无力。但我们同样应该看到，人类通过科学、医疗与合作展现出的惊人韧性。从疫苗的飞速研发到无数医护人员的无私付出，全球各地虽不停传来悲报，但也充斥着大量闪光的愿景。如果病毒当真不可战胜，那这些为之努力的强大意志又是什么呢？即便聚焦到个体，每个人也是竭尽所能的与病毒战斗到最后一刻，事实上大多数人确实是战胜了病毒。现代社会，科学的发展与医疗的进步，早已不是500年前的黑死病时期了。我并不否认悲观，也不否认人类的脆弱与局限性，但悲观并非应对危机的唯一态度，所有这一切都是有意义的，或许在不远的将来，人类终将战胜病毒也未可知。  就像游戏内那个假结局中，施德拉所说的那样：  [h1]“我们最后总会迎来终结，但不一定非得是现在，不一定非得在你手中。”[/h1]

GRIME is in my opinion one of the most underrated Metroidvania titles out there. While it may not be quite on par with Hollow Knight or the upcoming Silksong, I actually got much more enjoyment out of it than Ender Lilies: Quietus of the Knights despite that game being much more well known. It isn't flawless, but for the first game by Clover Bite it's an incredible showing.  To start with, the presentation here is excellent. While the visuals can lack a bit of polish at points, it has an overall incredibly striking art direction and morbid, abstract world which sets itself apart. Some areas are downright gorgeous, and while the game isn't quite top-notch as far as visual fidelity it still looks good overall.  The music is also absolutely fantastic, with most of the soundtrack being done by Alex Roe who is known for his excellent Dark Souls remixes. It's absolutely fantastic stuff, we're talking on the same level as the FromSoft titles that inspired him.  On the gameplay side it's also great, blending metroidvania with Dark Souls in many smart ways. The combat always requires you to be on your toes as enemies hit hard and deflecting/absorbing attacks is central to it. You're heavily rewarded for facing the enemies head-on rather than playing it safe, but managing your stamina means you always have to think and can't just spam the dash or be a glass cannon that kills bosses before they can do anything. Said bosses are also excellent, with distinct designs and ways of dealing with just about all of them, ramping up from relatively easy but not pushovers to some very demanding, but fair challenges that are very memorable.  You're also rewarded for good play with Ardor, which is essentially the inverse of how Hollow Knight handles death. Rather than losing currency on death, you lose a resource which acts as a currency multiplier, so the longer you go without dying the more you're rewarded, but you can't get stuck in a loop of dying and being too weak to do anything about it.  In typical Metroidvania fashion you explore the world, unlocking new abilities and finding new equipment to empower yourself as well as gain access to more of the world. Where it really becomes great is that in NG+ you keep everything, not just your level, but also the abilities and items. This makes  the game go from relatively linear to incredibly open, with almost the entire world accessible from the very start. What's more is that NG+ doesn't just increase the stats of enemies and bosses, it actually changes how almost all of them behave, giving them new properties or attacks as well as a NG+ exclusive secret boss. NG+ here actually feels like an extension of the base game and proper post-game, rather than an afterthought to make repeat playthroughs a little more interesting.  The story is also excellent, while it can be a bit confusing it's generally well told between various NPC dialogue, item descriptions and environmental storytelling. The setting is incredibly unique and dark, and it's engaging figuring out what exactly is going on in the world and what role the various characters and factions play in it. Without going into spoilers too much it's not just your typical "fallen kingdom, go take down the people responsible and choose to put the final nail in the coffin or keep it alive" type plotline.  The main negative here is the technical side. On launch the game was apparently horribly plagued with performance issues and bugs, and while it's gotten a lot better it's still not perfect. I encountered instances of clipping into terrain, randomly being teleported, and visual bugs. They were rare and didn't sour the experience, but it's clearly not the most polished game. The game also doesn't perform great, I can get a stable 180 FPS at native 1440p in games like Deep Rock Galactic, System Shock Remake, Doom Eternal, etc. at max or nearly max settings, but in this game I can't even hit 100 FPS with a Ryzen 7700X and RX 6800. It's apparently even worse on the Switch, with the game looking much worse and still performing horribly. For a first game it's somewhat excusable, but it's a blemish on an otherwise fantastic game regardless.  Aside from that my only major criticism is Traits & Hunt Points. While it's cool on paper and does allow for a decent amount of customization, they feel less like specializing into a certain playstyle and mostly are just enhancing stats and doing things you were already doing, not really living up to the concept of absorbing your foes and using their abilities. Giving you more damage/lifesteal on successful deflects or pulls, increasing stats, dealing damage to enemies on deflect, etc. The majority are just making you better at things you were already doing while pretending to be ways to change your playstyle. It feels inspired by the Power of Dominance from Castlevania: Aria of Sorrow & Dawn of Sorrow, but isn't executed quite as well as that or even the charms from Hollow Knight. They just aren't unique or distinct enough.  Overall though, GRIME is still a great Metroidvania I'd recommend to anyone who likes metroidvanias, action RPGs, or platformers. It stands out as something unique from a team with a strong vision and understanding of the genres. If I were to give it a score, I'd say 8/10 is about where it lands.

다크판타지 메트로배니아를 원하신다면 강력 추천드립니다. 스토리가 모호하고 세계관이 언제 망해도 괜찮을 것 같은 느낌은 소울류에서 많이 참고한 느낌입니다.  중후반부터 보스전 패턴보다 맵 기믹이 점점 어려워지더라구요.. 플랫폼 쪽은 굉장히 못하는 편인데  스토리와 엔딩은 너무 보고 싶어서 모드 도움도 받고 가이드도 보고 여러가지 참고 많이 해서 결국 깼습니다.. 가이드랑 모드 만들어주시는 분들 항상 감사드립니다..  게임 시스템에 패링이 필수기 때문에 각 먹이들의 공격 타이밍과 패턴은 계속 맞으면서 숙지해야합니다.. 다만 공격 자세 들어오는 순간 쳐내는 패링의 판정이 넉넉한 편이라고 생각이 들어요. 그리고 죽었을 때 마지막으로 들린 서로게이트에서 부활하게 될 시 잃는 아이템이나 자원이 거의 없기 때문에 여러번 부담없이 트라이가 가능합니다. 맵들이 개인차가 있겠지만 굉장히 빡센편이고 맵도 비컨 찾기 전까지는 깜깜해서 호불호가 갈릴 듯 합니다.  아쉬웠던 건 공격과 대쉬에 스테미나가 들어가서 여러번 타격해야하는 경우 굉장히 딜 타이밍이 빡세다는 점과  이동수단이 후반에서야 괜찮아지는 편이고 나머지는 뛰어야합니다... 다크소울처럼 맵이 유기적이긴한데 부분적입니다.  메트로베니아 플랫폼의 형태를 띄고있는 게임이어서 맵의 가로부분이 굉장히 긴 편이라 많이 뛰어야해요.  하지만 그 외에 세계관이나 해당장르쪽 게임들을 좋아하신다면 강력 추천드립니다. 멋진 게임이예요..

Really fun game, varied choice of weapons and abilities. I found the absorb/deflect (parry) mechanics easier than in other games which was very satisfying. Interesting levels and some bosses were challenging but not too challenging. I don't often play NG+ but I finished NG+ on Grime, founding playing through again with all abilities a real power trip and it was very fun! A lot of care and attention has gone into this game and a really high quality metroidvania!

游玩体验远超名气的惊喜之作，于我而言是渐入佳境式的体验，有种前中期难受叫苦后期渐渐让我打爽了然后畅快升华的感觉，个人觉得是一款非常优秀的类银河城，适合人群：类银熟手，对难度有一定耐性、对跳跳乐容忍度较高、喜欢弹反战斗体验的玩家。   在我看来，GRIME有着非常好的卖相，无论是外在的美术音乐还是内在的地图设计、战斗体系、技能与数值养成等，它都交出了一份让我满意的答卷。写一下自己感受深的几个方面：  首先，作为一款难度偏高、具有浓郁类魂特色的类银，制作组在战斗体系上下足了功夫，独特的吸收弹反贯彻始终，拉扯技能的新奇设计更是让人眼前一亮，吸收与拉扯极大地增强了战斗中与敌人的交互性；吸收敌人还能获取其能力，依靠个性化的技能加点来完成自己偏好的成长构架，并通过不同的衣服套装对技能进行加成；武器设计同样可圈可点，武器种类繁多却不空有其表，不同的攻击模组在战斗中的体验完全不同，武器流派选择与属性加点有机结合，主副武器的设置也有一定的搭配空间。  其次，GRIME对boss的设计我认为达到了同类游戏的顶尖水准，boss招式大开大合，打击感节奏感和可交互性强，主角核心战斗技能中的吸收与拉扯在boss战中大展身手，发挥了对boss招式进行打断并获得输出机会的高收益；boss战演出极为震撼，每个boss都具有自己的鲜明特色和美学设计，特效和光影演出一项不落，再加上boss战配乐制作水准极高，旋律黑暗华丽史诗，管弦乐团与人声合唱大气磅礴，配合画面演出起到很大加分作用。点名夸赞一周目特效最足的boss整合点秃鹫，最有创新和艺术气息的boss最终表演，以及作为终极排面的二周目boss梦诞恐魔，最后这位的设计和演出更是天花板级，不打二周目的人我会觉得没打到梦诞恐魔你就少玩了20块钱。  最后就顺势说说GRIME的多周目设计，我只打了一遍不完整的二周目，感觉可玩性还不错，等级、技能和收集品全部保留，也无需开信标点亮地图，很大程度上提供了二周目速通便利，方便迅速完成二周目成就以及径直去找二周目boss梦诞恐魔。多周目小怪和boss的招式有所变化和加强，部分boss甚至会多出一个阶段，战斗上带来一定程度的新奇感和难度。只是可惜武器升级材料有限，导致换武器有点困难，升级没跟上的武器多周目用起来会比较刮，多周目更换武器流派的空间不大，有点浪费了那么多的武器种类，如果武器升级和属性加点一样可以洗的话就好了。   一些个人感受到的小缺点：  1.最明显的一点是体力槽，理解制作组或许想要通过体力槽限制玩家的攻击和闪避频率从而鼓励弹反，但这点还是较大地影响了战斗的流畅性，战斗体系设计得如此精彩却被体力槽整得有点寸止，尤其在前期体力槽特别短并且玩家也没适应的时候打起来特别难受容易劝退，打boss时用空体力就站着挨打，不过中后期体力槽渐渐变长，恢复体力的速度和手段加强，玩家也逐渐懂得合理规划体力了，体力槽的劣势就渐渐被掩盖了。  2.跳跳乐比较多，初见跑图较为疲惫。不过作为一个对跳跳乐容忍程度较高的玩家，我个人觉得GRIME的跳跳乐手感还不错，关卡设计也挺流畅，比如说被遗忘的作品里后半段拿隐藏武器的跳跳乐我打得还挺爽的。  3.二段跳解锁时机太晚，二段跳是和空冲一样很影响跑图体验的重要位移技能，没有二段跳总觉得浑身刺挠，本作却把二段跳放在大后期，有点感觉是专门为了后期的跳跳乐关卡才设计的。  4.隐藏又多又深又繁琐，隐藏区域不一定在地图中标出。地上收集品那么多，但很多是无关紧要的消耗品，有些隐藏和需要经过一段跳跳乐才拿到的东西一看是消耗品或者武器锻造石就体验较差了。  5.新区域开荒无地图，点亮信标后才显示地图，作为补偿GRIME的地图会显示最近玩家走过的轨迹，一定程度上等于自己描绘了粗略的地图。   整体上GRIME是一部我非常喜欢的作品，是我通关之后会感到特别满足和回味的一款类银，非常期待续作GRIME2。

Had a lot of fun with this. Just a really solid soulsvania with a focus on parrying and surprisingly good platforming. Also works good on the deck just be sure to disable the touchscreen as unfortunately grazing it causes your controller to completely turn off and the game only accepts keyboard input  If you want to disable the touchscreen on the deck specifically for this game do the instructions below. Just copied it from reddit. I'd link it instead but steam is weird about that in reviews.  What you need to do is to go to your controller settings, then to the Action Sets section where you should have just the "Default" one. Tap the cog icon and select "Add Always-On command". Then you tap on the (greyed out) box that says "Add command" and you'll get the big menu with all the command types. The right-most section would be "System". There you'll look for a box that says "Touchscreen off'. Select that, apply, save and restart the game. No more issues.

Grime is a relatively enjoyable game to play, but there are just enough questionable moments to give me pause where a recommendation is concerned.  On a positive note, the art style is unique and I did enjoy that a lot. They very much went with trying to make everything feel as though it were assembled with real world modeling materials, and it worked, very well. Visually, no qualms. It goes far in selling the product, and not without good reason.  Unfortunately, this is where I will begin the negative points. The writing tries for the vague and surreal in every sentence and expression, with a hint of madness coming from some of the characters. This is likely subjective, but I used the word "tries" for a reason: none of the storytelling felt natural. It felt like someone sitting at a desk deliberately attempting to imitate a style without ever understanding why the Hollow Knight/Soulsborne writing worked in the first place. At no point did I feel like the player is presented with a firm grasp as to why they're doing anything that they are doing. It comes together somewhat at the end of the experience, but the overall story was presented so poorly that I barely cared by the time that I got there. I was interested in one thing: getting to that next goal indicated by a map marker.   The reason why opaque storytelling works in other games that came before, and I'll continue to use Hollow Knight and Dark Souls/Bloodborne as examples because they're popular and most easily identified, is because the game would start with a blank slate, and then quickly give the player some very general sense of motivation. "Oh, I need to work towards hatching this giant egg-like structure." "Ah! Bad things are happening, and I am to change it." The specifics as to the backstory and why everything in the world has taken place may be hidden in item descriptions, writings and conversations; but the goal is made clear.   None of that happens here, and the team should have given a motivation of any kind to the player character. Even minimalist stories like Shadow of the Colossus gave a little something (wake the girl). If it's ALL vague, then it feels a little too much like you're expecting the community to craft your tale for you. It's less about the fun of working together as a community to piece all of the extra bits together, and more trying to figure out why the character is there at all.  The weapons exist, and that is the best I can say of them. Most players will generally gravitate towards one and just stick with it. The game rarely encourages experimentation with different weapon types. Several players have stated that they stuck with the default starting weapon. Others, like myself, found Yr's Glaive early on and never really had much of a reason to change.  Armor is much the same. Full sets give SOME benefit, but it's so minor that it rarely registers as anything more than a minor convenience. You're mostly seeking visual flair with these sets, and wouldn't notice much difference if it wasn't there.  The fighting itself is simple to a fault: parry, dodge, hit, repeat. It FELT repetitive. Most games do tend to be repetitive, but can (and often do) hide it well. Here, the encounters do tend to just feel far too similar throughout with little variance.  The biggest issue for me is that the game felt very linear. Most games of this sort, exploration platformers, will have some semblance of linearity out of necessity. Ultimately there does need to be a beginning, middle and end. What you won't find here, though, that exists in other exploration platformers is much in the way of meaningful back doors into areas. The game tends to pretty strictly tell you which direction to go, and it doesn't give very much reason for you to even try to find another path (not that another path usually exists). I can see where the developers were inspired by the likes of Metroid, Hollow Knight, From Software. That said It's so much an A-to-B-to-C affair that I felt like the game would have been better served with specific stages in which one progresses from level 1 to level 2 and so on, with each stage being open enough to allow for the smattering of secret areas that exist. There wasn't much of a need for the connected world, at least in the way that this particular connected world was crafted.  You can backtrack eventually to get other things that had previously been left behind; but you're rarely given any real incentive to do so. Most of those things tend to be minor weapons (as above, weapons that you have no real need for) or minor items. Sometimes some armor.  The visuals and style do almost all of the heavy lifting, unfortunately. The rest of the game feels like ideas that wear their inspirations proudly, but without enough effort to answer the question of "what makes this fun?" or "what purpose does this serve to the experience?" More often than not, the answer just felt, to me, like a shrug followed by, "because we wanted it in there, I guess."

If you are looking for a challenging sidescroller soulslike metroidvenia with an "easy to learn hard to master" combat system and a lot of platforming elements, you came to the right place.  [h3] Combat [/h3] Combat does have some emphasis on parrying but definitely rewards you for mastering it so if you're into that it's a lot of fun. Dying does come with a penalty but it's way less punishing than a game like Dark Souls. There's a pretty big variety of weapons to find in the game with different kinds of special abilities which can spice up your combat style depending of preference.  [h3] Movement [/h3] Movement takes a sec to get used to but as you progress you unlock a ton of traversing abilities that unlock many platforming challenges which makes you want to explore and find all the MANY secrets this game has to offer.  [h3] Bosses [/h3] Really good variety of boss fights that never felt repetitive, also you get new skills from beating bosses so it makes you WANT to explore and find all of them.  [h3] Replayability [/h3] I really liked that NG+ gives enemies new attacks and makes the game harder by other means than just making them tanks, also NG+ adds a new boss (IMO hardest in the game).  Writing this review after completing NG and NG+ AND platinuming it. Gave this game a chance because it was on sale and I thought "why not" but it quickly became one of my favorites and was 100% worth my time.

[h2] Some sand in this game's gears [/h2]   There is definitely something with the world and vibe. The form-altering trope could have been taken a step further, with a malleable build for every situation instead of just a unconventional way of unlocking abilities. The lore is unformed, vaguely explained via the usual Cryptic Babble™️ completed by pseudo-expressive onomatopoeia. As in most Metroidvania games unfortunately. Apart from the omnipresent monotonous mooing/mumbling in some biomes, the sonic landscape is immersive and cohesive, with an ambient soundtrack well mixed in except when the strings get ahead of themselves. Which is rare. Just maybe a little too much crunch and munch ASMR.  Now when it comes to roaming this world, movement abilities don't really compensate the stiffness of the controls and platforming phases aren't really a treat. By way of combat, it's an anemic dodge & parry(absorb) loop you are forced into in order to unlock abilities. A shame, and possibly the most dry and gloomy aspect of this game's nicely crafted ambiance.

Grime is an interesting 2D souls-like/metroidvania type game, which I would almost put in the same vein as Hollow Knight (HK) and Nine Sols (NS). It definitely has a high emphasis on parrying and on boss fights, but compared to most other games shines with its dedication towards the NG+ experience. While I think the game needs a bit to get warm, when it hits, it hits you hard.   Another focus of Grime (similar to HK and NS) is the platforming. Which, like most 2D metroidvania games it takes some time to get good and really starts to make a lot of fun when you unlock most of your abilities. Grime finds a good balance in my opinion with neither being too hard or two easy, in terms of platforming. It does not reach the levels of HK path of pain (even though there is one section which seem to get a lot of inspiration from the path of pain), but it definitely harder than NSs platforming.   In terms of boss fights it is also not much behind the likes of HK or NS. It starts of good, being followed by some easier bosses, but towards the end gets a bit harder. And then the NG+ spices up everything again. The game doesn’t really shine in boss variety, but definitely in replayability of these bosses with increased difficulty. The determination towards replayability is the most fascinating thing about Grime. The devs really made the NG+ an interesting and worthwhile experience and didn’t just pump up all the enemies’ numbers like certain other devs (Fromsoftware). They upgrade every enemy in the game (or at least almost every enemy), to make their attacks a bit different and harder to avoid, added new phases to bosses and even made a challenging last boss fight only playable in the NG+. That’s real dedication. In addition, you keep all your movement abilities which makes traversing the map easier. It always bothered me when playing games like HK or NSs, that after finishing the game and starting a new character the start feels so slow compared to the end of the game. Since this option somewhat breaks some platforming in the beginning of the game, it seems to me this was implemented to cut down the time between boss fights, which were the real focus in the NG+ experience.   One, maybe a bit odd choice of Grime was to lock certain abilities behind optional boss fights. For example, the ability to warp between bon fires is only unlocked after defeating a boss which is not necessarily on the main path. The boss isn’t hard to find but it’s a bit strange that such a crucial ability is not after a mandatory boss fight. I believe this was done because some bosses and areas where added after the launch of the game, and maybe at the beginning the warp ability wasn’t implemented in the game (which probably lead to people complaining about backtracking… I would too). The way it is, exploration is definitely more rewarding since you are able to find very important things by doings so, but I can also see it being very frustrating if you miss abilities like warping.  To summarize, Grime maybe has some minor flaws and maybe lacks a bit in boss variety, but it definitely makes up to it by its dedication to people who enjoy its combat and boss fights. Its one of the best 2d souls-like games I played and almost reaches the upper echelon, that is Hollow Knight and Nine Sols.

Smooth gameplay with only a few bugs/hiccups. The combat is fun and the boss fights are just the right level of difficult to make victory really feel good. Exploration is good, but the map needs work... wandering around each zone blindly trying to find a beacon so you can keep some sense of location permanence is so stupidly frustrating.  The setting is surreal and intriguing, but there is not nearly enough to latch on to in regards to the story it is trying to tell. I respect the attempt to tell the greater story surrounding our character and the world they're born into through such a limited perspective, but this game really asks too much out of the player when it comes to unraveling the fabric of the plot and setting. The entire experience left me a little frustrated with how deliberately obfuscated it tries to be.  Otherwise I had a great time with it and look forward to the sequel.

Very good game. I found it a bit hard to grasp at the beginning, especially with the stamina ressource (the action you do like dash, attack or jump consume it and when you run out of stamina you have to wait for it to reload again) but once you get used to it it's pretty enjoyable to play with. One other nitpick i have is some progression points of the game were to me a bit unlogical so i'd suggest to check for a soluce/walkthrough if you struggle to know what you should do next at some points.  Other than that, the gameplay was very good, the art was really great, it took me around 20 hours to finish my first run and 38 to do all the achievements. New game + is pretty nice since it makes the game a bit harder, monsters and bosses have additional skills. The bosses have been the highlight of the game for me, they're pretty hard while still being fair, learning how they work to beat them has been a very nice experience.   To conclude if you like metroidvanias you should definitely like this game, the beginning might be a bit rough but it's worth it.

My final verdict for the game is a solid 8-9 out of 10. SOME SPOILERS AHEAD  PROS: -satisfying combat system -the parry is quite unique as it's both a defensive and offensive tool -the mobility skills are nice and play well around the concept of a "black hole" being -the exploration overall is pretty good, and rarely felt frustrating or uninspiring -bosses are nice to fight and master, and there are basically no runbacks (provided you unlocked available shortcuts) -customization is really nice, as you can combine weapon, armor set and traits that best suite your playstyle -the art direction is amazing and all areas have their own unique vibe -while the plot is still told the usual "souls way", the general theme of the game can be understood fairly easily -in terms of performance, the game played almost flawlessly for me with no bugs, glitches or crashes encountered during 2 playthroughs. -the NG+ retains mobility skills, allowing you to sequence break and reach all areas much faster; enemies have an "enchanced moveset" (bosses as well), which encourages replayability. -For platform fans, the last update introduce a "Path of Pain" area that rewards players with a very cool (and unique) weapon that does a lot of damage.   CONS -Apart from 2 weapons and the ability to further power up weapons and traits, the NG+ doesn't seem to offer much -The NG+ exclusive boss is really fun to master, but the fact he doesn't drop his weapon (but just his armor set, which has NO USEFUL PASSIVE ABILITY) was quite a letdown -The game breaks a staple of the genre by offering 2 endings which can be obtained regardless of how much you explored into the game, and considering all the stuff going on with the Child, that seems like wasted potential to me. -The fact that both the sprint and walk action are tied to the same input means that in order to change them you have to use a pearl to remove the trait otherwise you won't be able to sprint (run).

Grime - метроидванию-соулслайк со своей уникальной атмосферой всепоглощающего разрушения и упадка в который вы падаете, чтобы понять, постичь и поглотить всё до чего сможет дотянуться ваша любопытная чёрная дыра  Хорошая метроидвания с прикольными локациями и со своими достаточно интересными и не сложными механиками (но если вы захотите заполучить МЕЧ-КОСУ, то ваша вера в адекватность разрабов может чуть пошатнуться)  Достойный представитель соулслайков с хорошими боссами и со своими по истину новыми механиками, где поглощение ни в коем случае не копирка парирование, а нечто новое, хоть и недалеко отходит от него по смыслу, также ещё можно отметить притягивание, что тоже достаточно забавная штука  Самое главное - это навыки и способы их приобретения в этой игре, типо "Перед тобой ходит вкусная и опасная бяка которую можно съесть,  а в ней валяется навык на более приятно съедение всех остальных", и бяка выполнила свою цель, и вы сыты  Я рекомендую это игру, у неё есть свои косяки, но их не так много и будем надеяться что продолжение сможет развить идеи и механики первой части, ну а пока что можно пойти в Rain World, они как раз в комплекте продаются...

重新通关了一边新版grime之后，我只能说这是一款非常复杂的类银河战士恶魔城游戏，它有它独特的、出色的一面：比如美术、世界观、无缝地图、部分boss设计，但他也有相对糟糕的一面：跳跳乐的设计、后期部分boss设计、指引问题等等。鉴于我希望以差评警醒制作组和试图入坑的类银河战士恶魔城新人玩家，我这里主要介绍一下糟糕的那一面： 1、跳跳乐设计，在dlc之后，制作组失心疯般在本作加入了大量跳跳乐环节，我认为这是一大败笔，首先是本作的跳跃手感不算出色，面对需要攀爬边缘以及钩爪的时候尤为明显，模糊的攀爬和陷阱判定以及谜一样的无法更改的摇杆钩爪都使得本作的跳跳乐体验在手感这一块已经落入下风，然而更糟糕的是本作的跳跳乐关卡设计。平心而论本作的跳跳乐绝对不算难，但是流程设计的极其无趣，以本作的“苦痛之路”未完成的作品一关为例，最前面一关是左右来回六个左右的攀爬梯子躲定时出来的刺，触碰到刺从头开始，就是这开头给予了想要挑战本作跳跃大关卡的玩家一个当头棒喝：集合了重复度高、容错率低、操作手感差等众多缺点于一体，昭示了后面这又臭又长的跳跃大关的全部缺点。看到二代宣传片似乎还有不少跳跳乐环节，我个人其实并不反感类银的跳跃关卡，但是还是希望制作组精进一下设计水准，如果二代的跳跃关和一代一样差劲的话会成为二代口碑的一个伏笔。 2、boss设计问题：本作的boss设计问题几乎全部集中于后期几个大场面boss。相信玩过游戏的玩家都会一致认为本作的战斗系统核心为弹反动作，这个弹反动作兼具了造成硬直、输出、回复血量与体力等几大作用于一体，是grime最核心也是最出色的动作，然而可能是制作组有意限制玩家后期性能强大的弹反动作，几个大场面boss几乎没有给你可供弹反的动作，或者是可以弹反的动作模糊不清难以被玩家辨识。这样设计大大降低了玩家攻克boss时的容错率、交互感，使得打boss几乎变成了“如何躲过boss的一个个招式，并找到输出间隙a几下”的无趣流程。我认为前期几个boss的设计水平明明非常不错，保持了一定难度下的同时围绕弹反做的交互感也提高了玩家的战斗体验。后期由于性能膨胀而丢掉弹反这一设计完全是捡了芝麻丢了西瓜。 3、引导问题：这里主要集中在世界传送这一能力的指引问题，作为可以说是非常关键的能力，作者将它完全丢在了一个与前中期主线指引南辕北辙的支线地方，我个人在第一次游玩的时候是直接与这个能力完全错失，导致我玩了近20h都未能获取传送能力。这么设计的原因我只能解释为两种：一是作者不希望玩家在前中期拿到这个能力，希望玩家在后期进入该地图时才可使用传送能力。二是作者认为传送能力并非类银的地图设计里所包含的必要能力，于是塞入了地图一隅当作探索支线的奖励。无论是哪种解释我都不认为是一个当下游戏设计正确的思路，传送能力即使不作为自带能力也应该作为靠早期一点的主线中出现的一个必要收集，否则玩家的探图体验会极其糟糕。

Wow. I had not heard of Grime before I played it.  I had it included in a Humble Bundle and thought I'd give it a shot.   Turns out, it is one of my all-time favourite platform games, right up there with Ori and the Blind Forest.  Brilliant level design coupled with a thoughtful upgrade system and a wacky story are the supports for some of the best platforming mechanics I've ever played.  The level of precision in both the platforming and the combat is almost second to none (least none that I've played).  Part Ori and part Metroid, this is an experience I'll never forget.  Yes, it is tough but it is exquisitely balanced and fair.  I never had to look up anything online for help and what seemed like initially insurmountable bosses became some of the coolest pattern-recognition fights I've ever done.   Highly recommended for those who like to pull out their hair and scream at their screen.  As I said though, the level of polish and balance takes a frustrating encounter and turns it into a sublime dance of destruction.  There are very few Metroidvania games on the level of Grime, don't miss out on this one.

A Hidden Gem with Raw Style and Brutal Beauty  Just wrapped up Grime, and I have to say—this game genuinely surprised me in the best way. The world design is surreal and captivating, offering a unique atmosphere that kept pulling me forward. While I wish some of the zone themes had more memorable tracks (especially compared to titles like Hollow Knight or Metroid), the soundtrack still hits hard where it matters most. The boss themes in particular are fantastic—intense, evocative, and fitting for the fights they accompany.  Speaking of bosses, Dreamborn Terror was a standout. An absolutely brutal battle, but one of the most exhilarating experiences I’ve had in an action-platformer. I think it took me around 30–40 attempts to finally win, and the first time I made it to phase 4 (the final phase) and died, it tilted me hard. Still, the satisfaction of finally beating it was incredible.  The Kinship ending was a very satisfying conclusion, and added a thoughtful layer to the journey. Combat overall feels solid—maybe a touch sluggish at times—but the unique mechanics and thoughtful progression make up for it. The Hunt Point system is a great touch, giving you more reason to engage deeply with the world and its enemies.  If you’re into atmospheric, difficult action-platformers with strong boss design and a rich world, Grime is well worth your time. It’s not without rough edges, but what it offers is memorable and absolutely worth experiencing.

______Настолько понравилась игра, что закрыл на 100%. Уникальная игра, грамотный пример того, как надо делать метроидванию-соулслайк. Уникальный визуальный стиль, дизайн просто офигенный, некоторые боссы выглядят как будто это реальные фигурки из камня или глины, которых реально сделали, а потом анимировали. Некоторые локации вообще вауэффект с детальными задними планами и т.д.  ______Построение локаций с точки зрения исследования, скрытых мест, шорткатов и всего такого тоже на высоте. Локация Дворец вытесанных просто удивляет своей изобретательностью и платформинг секциями. Иногда непросто найти путь, но исследовать все это очень интересно, да и в Hollow Knight тоже были моменты когда не знал куда идти).  ______Боевка и прокачка супер, вот казалось бы соулслайк, что мы там не видели, но здесь все как-то по-новому играется вкупе с местной прокачкой. Например парирование здесь, не просто станет врага, но и наносит ему урон и восполняет выносливость, уворот-дэш сквозь врага также при должной прокачке оставляет след, при вражеском ударе по которому, враг опять получает урон и т.д. Таким образом при должном умении и соответствующей прокачке, бой с боссом может превратиться в бесконечное нанесение урона по нему, несмотря на то, уворачиваешься ты или атакуешь. Атаки врагов разного типа, что-то парируется, что-то дэшится, есть эффект, когда врага можно притянуть выбив из равновесия, все грамотно подсвечивается для информативности. Боссы здесь сложные, но честные. Потратив немного времени и поняв, как он атакует, ты уже выстраиваешь тактику, оттачиваешь мувы, чтоб избегать урона и вот уже почти победил).  ______Еще про прокачку, здесь есть интересная система пассивок, которые ты получаешь, поглощая несколько врагов определенного типа, сперва открывается сама пассивка, но чтоб она заработала в нее надо вкладывать специальные очки прокачки. Так вот, этих пассивок прям много и самое интересное, что почти все полезные. Вкачать все явно не получится, приходится выбирать(кстати в любой момент можно пересобрать билд за внутриигровую валюту), как по мне здесь можно очень по-разному прокачаться, некоторое билдостроение присутствует. Прогрессия от прокачки ощущается, но и скилл тут также необходим, чем дальше по игре идешь, тем увереннее становишься). Также на НГ+ прокачка продолжает прогрессировать, причем не просто поднимаешь статы, а появляются два новых ресурса, позволяющих дальше качать оружие и пассивки, когда проходишь первый раз максимум по оружию +6 и по пассивкам до пяти уровней, во второй оружие можно доточить до +9, а также усиливать пассивки за пределами пяти уровней.  ______Есть много разного оружия под статы со своими уникальными приемами, есть необычные варианты, но из всего, что есть в игре, пушки это наверное то, что меньше всего удивляет. Они есть, они хорошие, они работают. Также в лучших традициях сосаликов постепенно узнаешь детали местной истории: странный враждебный мир, странные персонажи, загадочный лор, все включено в общем.  ______В итоге: Как по мне, это шедевр, это прям свежо и интересно играется. Да игра сложная, но повторюсь, честная, отсюда возникает только одно желание - преодолевать дальше. Из всех соулслайков в которые играл - этот, для меня, наверное лучший. Как метроидвания игра также великолепна. Жду теперь вторую часть.
刚进去的时候感觉画质有点粗糙，但画风很符合自己的品味，游戏特色是吸收和拉扯，boss战演出和机制不错，打起来很有爽感。后面地图有很多跳跳乐感觉难度也还好，唯一长且难度高的就一个，最后获得的镰型剑配上吸血装真好用。隐藏是真多呀，全靠官方wiki上的互动地图和攻略。游戏推荐多周目游玩。

[h3]Grime Review – A Masterpiece Carved in Darkness[/h3]  Grime is not just a video game. It’s an experience that strikes the senses, stirs the soul, and leaves a lasting mark. A journey through an abstract and surreal world where every element – from the music to the environments – is crafted with raw, visceral beauty that feels almost tangible.  Visually, Grime is a work of art. Every area seems hand-sculpted, alive and breathing, as if part of an ancient, forgotten universe. The dark tones, the impossible architecture, the eerie creatures – everything contributes to building a world that is both haunting and mesmerizing.  The gameplay is solid, deep, and rewarding. The combat system is original and engaging, with the absorb mechanic adding a unique and strategic twist to the genre. Every fight demands focus, timing, and nerves of steel. Every victory feels genuinely earned and deeply satisfying.  Exploration is clever and never dull. Levels are intricately connected, rewarding curiosity and a good sense of direction. Abilities unlock naturally and open up new paths, making every discovery feel meaningful.  The soundtrack is hypnotic – it never overwhelms but enhances the atmosphere and blends perfectly with the game’s aesthetic. There are moments where you just stop, to listen and take it all in.  Grime is one of those rare titles that manages to be both intimate and spectacular. A dark, yet stunningly beautiful journey that speaks of transformation, identity, and resilience.  A game you won’t forget. A world that changes you. A work of art that deserves to be experienced.  Score: 10/10

And if you die from the cheapest enemy positioning or any of the mentioned problems, you are brought back to where you were 10 minutes ago, and it's the *first time* this problem makes me *not* want to explore, a really important and fun thing on Metroidvanias.  Honestly, I really wanted to play this since I heard it was a good and challenging game, but there's a difference between challenging and just plain annoying.

I think this game is great, and is probably one of the best metroidvanias out there if you like Dark Souls.  I'm not one of those people so I wasn't able to enjoy myself as much as I could have.  In fact, this was one of those rare games where I straight-up was just not having fun and I was getting ready to straight-up quit.  However, at one of the earlier bosses, something clicked when it came to the sort of rhythm of the combat that made me realize how I was supposed to play it.  I liked it!  However, I'm still not the biggest fan of some of its Dark Souls influences.  The inventory and equipment system wasn't my favorite.  Leveling up didn't really feel super satisfying all the time either, and the aesthetics/story were not in my wheelhouse either.  Still, the gameplay did make up for all this.  I didn't hate this game by any means.    Overall, I had fun with this game, but I didn't love it.  It's clearly one of the most polished games I've played in the genre, and its heavy ties to Dark Souls do a lot to set it apart from much of the genre.  Usually if a metroidvania is a soulslike, it will often just leave your currency/exp on the map for you to go and fetch on death.  I gotta respect that it goes all-in, even if I didn't fully resonate with this game overall.

[h2]Phenomenal 2D Metroidvania - Miles and miles above most others.[/h2]  [h3]The Good[/h3] Grime has massive ambition, slowly unravelling to reveal a more than excellent Metroidvania. The setting is truly bizarre and you learn early on that you're going to get something quite unique... You play as a 'proportioned' being fighting and absorbing more malformed beings. The combat is kinda souls like, with a heavy focus on parrying. It is unforgiving and you will die a lot so if that isn't your bag this game may not be for you.  Apart from the art style, everything else in this game gets slowly (and I mean [b]slowly[/b]) dripped down to you--Weapons, Armours, Abilities, Map, Story. It's a slow burner for sure and I can see why a large chunk of people tap out early but the game is definitely better for it. The prominent vibe I got from Grime is that it doesn't treat you like a kid and it expects you to work things out for yourself--there are so many subtle nuanced things in combat and platforming that you just end up stumbling across and it's super engrossing. There are a million different weapons with special abilities for you to choose from that work with different stat spreads, allowing you to suit the combat to your style. There's upgrades and consumables and...yeah, its kinda souls like.  For me, the combat came second to the exploration which is where Grime shines best. There are SO MANY secrets to uncover and the movement system through platforming feels really fresh and intricate towards the end. I might be misremembering how hard Celeste was but there is a section in Grime that harked similar feelings.   The story is pretty bleak and mostly just hinted at rather than revealed, but the atmosphere is thick. Depressing but in a good way.  [h3]The Not As Good[/h3] Both the combat and the platforming have a little bit of jank. For the most part they're very snappy and fluid, but you do get frustrated trying to account for the jank from time to time. Something that you can definitely get used to.  I'm far from a masochist, but I did feel like all the bosses were a little easy. You get crushed pretty easily but once you figure out what they're doing it's not hard at all. There was no boss where I thought, "Wow, that was crazy" after beating them.  [h3]Should you play it?[/h3] If you enjoy sombre worlds, platforming off the beaten path or curious mythos I would recommend this as a must-play. There has been a sequel announced and I think it is going to be really really special.

Grime is a masterpiece. It stands alongside metroidvania classics such as Hollow Knight, Blasphemous, and Ori, thanks to its mysterious world, surreal art style, and atmospheric soundtrack. The environments feel eerie, sad, and oddly beautiful all at once.  Combat is tight and rewarding, with a unique absorption mechanic and a solid variety of weapons, skills and armor sets that allow for multiple builds. Bosses are challenging but fair, and a New Game+ mode offers added difficulty for those seeking more.  If you enjoy cryptic storytelling, engaging exploration, and satisfying combat, Grime is for you.

Totally recommend this game. I love the aesthetics and atmosphere. Character build variety with different weapons and fighting styles. Engaging and challenging combat and platforming. Alluring story and music.

* Great parry system - instant, consumes no stamina, heals and/or makes enemies vulnerable. Parry is a first class citizen here and without mastering it, one will not get far. * Good variety in weapons, armor and character upgrades. * Combat is short, impactful and satisfying - no fight ever outstays its welcome. * The game world concept is weird and fresh -  it is mostly roots, rocks, plants and other inaminate objects are given sentient life against their will, and most of them suffering as a result. The only true fully-developed civilization is a doomsday cult eagerly awaiting the protagonist's arrival. * Excellent soundtrack, especially during boss fights. * Native Steam Controller support with a preset that works great out of the box.

首先，战斗系统太有迷惑性了，大量的武器和动作模组，结果你告诉我这是格挡模拟器？如果你按魂游思路翻滚平A循环，这游戏不出一个小时你就玩吐了，但是你去玩“吸收”格挡你会觉得柳暗花明。  这游戏设计太矛盾了，很明显“吸收”是主要机制，很多设计就是为“吸收”准备的，简单一个例子就是你是没有正常远程输出手段的，只有吸收才能处理远程小怪，并且如果你用武器尝试交互，很多BOSS根本打不动，但是用吸收瞬间难度下降一大截，你能明显感受到吸收才是交互的设计起点，因此，武器系统整个就是吸收的陪衬，系统本身根本撑不起作为一个玩法的体验，但是武器系统是分流派的，换句话说武器是想做成玩法体验的，所以每当第二次打开游戏拾起吸收的我回想起第一次用武器交互的折磨经历，我都想说制作组你们太幽默了。   但是话又说回来了，明白游戏的主要玩法后，游戏的战斗体验怎么样，很不错，格挡的手感不算难，判定也比较宽松，基本看到抬手就可以吸，并且小怪包括BOSS都是招数很少的，练习成本偏低，唯一的难点就是血量给的太吝啬，回血手段有限而且极其不便，要是回血是正常药瓶回血，这游戏难度也不至于劝退很多人，探图问题也可以得到一定程度改善。想说的就这些，祝你愉快

这个游戏就如同其中的主角一般，外表独特而又神秘，可是内部只有不安和混乱。游戏缺点具体如下： 1.体力条的设计是一大败笔，除了徒增游戏难度没有任何意义 2.回血机制存在问题，角色的回血需要靠弹反挣得“天息”才能回血，而本作主打的弹反手感一般，怪却个个是快慢刀高手，总是陷入越没血，越弹反，然后死亡的恶性循环中。 3.存档点稀少，探图成本过于高昂。 4.武器种类多样，但大部分的打击感都一般。 5.魂的设计部分比较肤浅，缺少魂游那种独特的魅力。 6.NPC的角色塑造非常一般，BOSS的招式设计也比较一般，难以留下深刻印象。 7.主角能力的解锁不符合直觉，二段跳这种基本能力居然放到后期才解锁。 当然游戏也有优点，比如： 1.地图的设计非常有水平，尤其是在地图的连通性上。 2.美术风格比较有特色，游戏最后的几个场景很吸引眼球。 但总而言之，游戏缺点已经严重的破坏了游戏性，难受和红温贯穿了游戏的全程，再三考虑后还是决定给予差评。

Legitimately one of the best soulslike/metroidvania experiences I've ever had, with possibly the best environment design I've seen. Unsettling atmosphere, locations, and enemies, solid and responsive combat, deeply satisfying combat, and great dialogue/class commentary to top it all off. Minor jank here and there, a few frustrating portions, but nothing major. I continue to go back and forth between a 9/10 and a 10/10, because the good FAR outweighs the bad and provides a unique, challenging, and refreshing approach to both the soulslike and metroidvania genres.

If you don’t like Soulslike games, then stay away. Otherwise, you should enjoy GRIME. I did.  + Dying doesn’t make you lose your mass (souls). Only Andor which helps gain more mass. + Controls are pretty good on XBOX controller. + Falling down from a great height or stepping your toes on spikes doesn’t instantly kill you unless you are at a very low health. You respawn where you last stepped on solid ground. This is a huge positive. + You’re going to die a lot, especially on bosses. Observe. Know when to parry. Don’t force your attacks unless they’re completely open. +- You liked the first phase? What about a second phase? +- Pull mechanic is a little disorienting. For myself anyway. It took awhile to understand it. +- Respeccing costs 5 Motley Pearls for stats and 1 Motley Pearl for traits. These are limited. +- Starts off easy and then ramps up hard.  Fast travel between Surrogates (Bonfires) only exists after you beat a certain optional boss at the midpoint of the game. It’s like Dark Souls 1 with the Lordvessel. You can still fast travel via Nerveroot early game, but you have to walk to those things.  Music is pretty dang good.  Story is ? / 10. You probably won’t care for it. Even after beating the game, I’m still confused as to what is going on.   Score: 9 out of 10.
